capture.cameraPose: Desktop Chrome DevTools viewport after adding visible Quest Evidence row summaries for hand representation, locomotion attempt, and trace attempt.
capture.captureCommand: Chrome DevTools MCP reloaded http://localhost:5173/, waited for visible hand rep and attempt text, verified zero console errors/warnings, then captured a viewport screenshot.

notes: This slice improves operator evidence visibility only; the ED bay, patient, staff, family, and equipment remain placeholder-quality in the desktop preview.
The visible evidence now names missing or primitive hand representation, but no actor mesh, clothing, skin, rigging, or device realism improvement is proven here.
The operator-facing Quest Evidence rows visibly summarize hand representation, locomotion attempt, and trace attempt without requiring JSON scrolling.
The capture proves desktop UI visibility of attempt summaries, not physical headset input, locomotion comfort, or headset select latency.
The added summary text fits inside the operator panel rows at the desktop viewport and does not overlap adjacent controls or clinical context panels.

This is desktop Chrome DevTools evidence only; it is not physical Quest proof, not immersive WebXR proof, not in-headset readability proof, not hand-tracking quality proof, and not production runtime readiness.
